item.title: Pentagon Announces First Three U.S. Deaths in War With Iran
item.description: U.S. Central Command did not say where the troops were killed but added that five others had been seriously wounded and that several had shrapnel injuries.

item.title: Iran Attacks Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar and Bahrain After U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: Iran responded to an American and Israeli assault by attacking Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar and Bahrain, threatening those states’ reputations for security and openness.

item.title: UK troops and civilians in Middle East put at risk by Iran strikes, Healey says
item.description: British military personnel at a base in Bahrain were "within several hundred yards" of a strike, the defence secretary says.

item.title: After Attack on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, See Photos and Videos From the Middle East
item.description: Here are images from Iran, Israel and elsewhere in the Middle East.

item.title: Israelis Are Tense but Relieved That Iran’s Supreme Leader Is Dead
item.description: Iranian missile and drone attacks have caused anxiety across Israel, but many believe it is a price worth paying for killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: How will strikes on Iran affect global energy flows?
item.description: Tehran has previously threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint

item.title: Trump’s epic gamble in the Middle East
item.description: The US president’s goal of regime change augurs immense regional chaos

item.title: At least 153 dead after reported strike on school, Iran says
item.description: Iran has blamed the US and Israel for the strike; the US is looking into reports of the incident, while the IDF says it is "not aware" of operations in the area.

item.title: The U.S.-Israeli attacks have killed a number of Iranian leaders.
item.description: The United States and Israel killed many senior Iranian leaders in the .

item.title: OPEC Plus to Boost Oil Production as Iran Strikes Threaten Price Spike
item.description: The cartel pledged to increase output by 206,000 barrels a day from next month, and said that members would “closely monitor and assess market conditions.”

item.title: ‘It’s Surreal’: Iranians in Disbelief After Supreme Leader Assassinated
item.description: Large crowds of people celebrated the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in American and Israeli strikes, while many others gathered to mourn.

item.title: Iran’s Regime May Survive, but the Middle East Will Be Changed
item.description: A badly weakened Iran will no longer intimidate or threaten its neighbors in the same way. The regional impact could be comparable to the collapse of the Soviet Union.

item.title: Nine dead in missile attack on Israel as Iran strikes region
item.description: Several deaths are reported across the Middle East as Tehran retaliates for massive strikes by the US and Israel.

item.title: Oil prices forecast to jump despite Opec+ pledge to raise output
item.description: Analysts say Iran conflict likely to increase cost of crude by 5-15% when markets reopen on Sunday

item.title: The months of planning behind US-Israeli mission to target Iran's supreme leader
item.description: A piece of crucial intelligence arrived just hours before the attack on a compound in central Tehran.

item.title: In maps: Strikes across Iran and the Middle East
item.description: Israel and the US have launched strikes across Iran, with Iran retaliating with strikes across the region.

item.title: Iran war will test the vulnerability of oil markets
item.description: While there are buffers of supply, spare capacity will come under pressure in the event of any further escalation

item.title: In Milan, fashion in the shadow of war
item.description: As Dolce & Gabbana, Bottega Veneta and Armani mused on brand identity, world events cast a pall on fashion week

item.title: Air travel heavily disrupted following Iranian strikes
item.description: Tens of thousands of travellers left stranded

item.title: South Africa beat Zimbabwe to set up NZ semi-final
item.description: South Africa produce an unconvincing performance with the bat as they beat Zimbabwe by five wickets to set up a semi-final against New Zealand

item.title: What will war in Iran do to the global economy?
item.description: Conflict has exposed how much growth depends on energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Ali Khamenei may be dead, but Donald Trump has unfinished business
item.description: The president has declared bombing will continue until it brings “peace”

item.title: What we know so far about the US-Israel attacks and Iran's retaliation
item.description: Iran responds by launching missiles and drones towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries that host US military bases.

item.title: China hopes IVF can slow its baby bust
item.description: That may be overambitious

item.title: India, the world’s most colourful country, is changing its hues
item.description: Its transformation can be seen in its evolving palette

item.title: The U.S. Released the Epstein Files. The Arrests Are Overseas.
item.description: While the latest tranche of documents about the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has exposed a deeply American scandal, it is Europe where the aggressive enforcement is taking place.

item.title: Who Could Take Over for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei?
item.description: Now that Iran has declared that its supreme leader is dead, it is unclear which of his possible successors might rise.

item.title: Lyse Doucet: This is an extraordinary moment Iran has been preparing for
item.description: The killing of the supreme leader will be a huge jolt to the Islamic Republic, which will try hard to show it has a plan in place.

item.title: Inside Iran, panic as strikes hit but for some it's a moment of relief
item.description: Iranians talk about what is happening inside the country, despite an almost total internet blackout.

item.title: In Ukraine, a Community of ‘Simple Believers’ Shuns the Modern World
item.description: The Christians known as viruiuchi prostaky see electricity, cars, higher education and much else as distractions from what really matters.

item.title: How Starmer can make student loans ‘fairer’
item.description: The central question for the UK prime minister and his chancellor is who benefits from the different policy options

item.title: With the supreme leader dead, power in Iran hangs in the balance
item.description: A dramatic blow to the regime—followed by dangerous uncertainty

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader killed in joint US Israeli strikes, Trump says
item.description: Tehran responded to the strikes with a wave of missiles and drones aimed at Israel and Gulf countries that host US bases.

item.title: Bowen: A dangerous moment, but US and Israel see opportunity not to be missed
item.description: Israel and the US believe Iran's regime is vulnerable, dealing with an economic crisis and the aftermath of protests.

item.title: Ayatollah Khamenei's iron grip on power in Iran comes to an end
item.description: Iranian state TV confirms the supreme leader has been killed on the first day of massive US and Israeli air strikes on the country.

item.title: Why is WhatsApp's privacy policy facing a legal challenge in India?
item.description: A 2021 policy update requires users to share data with Meta for ads purposes to continue using the app.

item.title: Where have Nepal's 'nepo kids' gone as corruption takes centre stage in election?
item.description: The excess of politicians' children was front and centre of the anger that drove protests that toppled the government last year.

item.title: Minister Josh Simons resigns after Labour Together claims
item.description: Simons faced claims the think tank he used to run commissioned a report which looked into the background of journalists.

item.title: Burnham would probably have won by-election, Labour deputy leader says
item.description: Lucy Powell tells the BBC that Labour needs to make more use of the Greater Manchester mayor.

item.title: America’s Gulf allies face a moment of great peril
item.description: If war cannot be avoided, should they take sides?

item.title: Trump Gives Green Light to Private Oil Sales to Cuba
item.description: After blocking foreign oil shipments, the Trump administration is now allowing small amounts of oil to enter Cuba — as long as they circumvent government hands.

item.title: A Trump Call Ignited Saudi-U.A.E. Feud
item.description: A request made to President Trump about the war in Sudan is at the heart of a diplomatic dispute between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
item.description: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.

item.title: America and Israel bomb Iran, aiming to topple its regime
item.description: Donald Trump calls on Iranians to rise up. War could quickly engulf the Middle East

item.title: Mahmood to press on with immigration reforms despite by-election defeat
item.description: The home secretary is to double down on plans for Danish-style restrictions on asylum seekers.

item.title: The Bloody Rise and Fall of Mexico’s Top Crime Boss
item.description: El Mencho’s brutality and business acumen put him atop the cartel world, until he made a fatal mistake.

item.title: Carney Visiting India, Australia and Japan to Build Canada’s ‘Middle Power’ Bonds
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney visits India, Australia and Japan seeking deals to strengthen his country’s links to Indo-Pacific powers and break Canada’s dependence on the United States.

item.title: “Everyone is Overreacting” on the Tariff Ruling, Stephen Vladeck Says
item.description: Is the Supreme Court really checking Trump’s power?

item.title: Taiwan Arms Sale Approved by Congress Is Delayed as Trump Plans Visit to Beijing
item.description: The package worth billions of dollars and endorsed by lawmakers is stalled at the State Department as the U.S. and China plan an April summit.

item.title: These filmmakers know exactly how to get you hooked on bizarre one-minute dramas
item.description: The business depends on packing a punch right away because the first five to 10 episodes are free.

item.title: Aid groups in Gaza and West Bank thrown lifeline as Israel court pauses ban threat
item.description: Dozens of well-known groups faced shutdown under a new Israeli law, although the reprieve might be temporary.

item.title: Drone jammed near French aircraft carrier was Russian, says Sweden
item.description: Sweden's prime minister said it was a serious but not unexpected incident - and a "Russian way of acting that we recognise from other places".

item.title: Starmer vows to fight on after historic Green by-election win
item.description: The result - in a seat Labour has held for nearly 100 years - heaps further pressure on the PM's position.

item.title: Tram derails in Milan, leaving two dead and dozens injured
item.description: The crowded tram was travelling in the centre of Milan at rush hour when it appeared to crash into the side of a building.

item.title: What does “open war” between Pakistan and Afghanistan amount to?
item.description: The bombing of military sites in Kabul and Kandahar marks a sharp escalation in tensions

item.title: Some Russians Go Without Heat or Power in Winter, as Energy System Falls Into Disrepair
item.description: A dozen major blackouts and central heating cutoffs across the country have affected hundreds of thousands during a brutal winter.

item.title: Reform UK reports by-election 'family voting' concerns to police
item.description: It comes after election observers raised concerns about "extremely high" levels of family voting.

item.title: Private schools lose legal challenge over VAT changes
item.description: A group of low-fee paying private schools have challenged the government's removal of the VAT exemption.

item.title: Plaid Cymru vs Reform is choice between culture or ignorance, says ap Iorwerth
item.description: Plaid Cymru's leader told the party's conference that a Reform Welsh government would set Wales back by decades.

item.title: Were Duterte’s Speeches Orders to Kill or Hyperbole?
item.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have heard starkly different interpretations this week of the words of former President Rodrigo Duterte of the Philippines.

item.title: Palestine Action arrest as Churchill statue defaced
item.description: The Westminster statue of the former prime minister is vandalised with slogans daubed in red paint.

item.title: Pakistan Strikes Afghanistan in ‘Open War’ Against Taliban Regime
item.description: The airstrikes came hours after Afghan troops had attacked Pakistani border positions and follow months of worsening relations between the neighboring countries.

item.title: Government to give go-ahead for £1bn defence helicopter deal
item.description: The chancellor has stepped in to protect jobs at the helicopter manufacturer in Somerset, the BBC understands.

item.title: At least 55 Ghanaians killed in Russia-Ukraine war, minister says
item.description: This is the highest number of confirmed casualties from a single African country in the Ukraine-Russia war.

item.title: Henry Zeffman: Green victory shows insurgent parties are here to stay
item.description: The party's historic win in Gorton and Denton by-election is clearest sign yet of changing political landscape.

item.title: Hungary Plays Spoiler in Europe as Orban Strains for Votes at Home
item.description: Facing a serious election challenge, Prime Minister Viktor Orban is holding up a big E.U. loan for Ukraine. Analysts say the timing is no coincidence.

item.title: Brazil’s almighty Supreme Court must win back public trust
item.description: Recent scandals have tarnished the image of one of Brazil’s most important democratic institutions

item.title: Why are Afghanistan and Pakistan fighting?
item.description: The two sides have different versions of how and why the deadly dispute began.

item.title: Spanish opposition calls for disgraced ex-monarch to return from exile
item.description: The 88-year-old Juan Carlos should spend his last years in dignity, the leader of Spain's main opposition party says.

item.title: What we know after latest escalation in Pakistan-Afghanistan tensions
item.description: Bombings by Pakistan are the most significant development in tensions between the two neighbours - though the physical impact remains unclear.

item.title: Why Trump means the Cuban Revolution faces its biggest threat yet
item.description: Will a worsening internal crisis create the conditions for the Cuban Revolution to unravel from within?

item.title: From plumber to Parliament: the Green Party's new MP
item.description: Spencer marked her victory by apologising to customers for potentially having to cancel work due to her Westminster move.

item.title: Former US diplomat sentenced to life for abusing two girls in Burkina Faso
item.description: He used the girls' mother's life-threatening illness as an opportunity to demand sex, said the US Office of Public Affairs.

item.title: Miliband says climate impact of data centres is uncertain
item.description: The admission comes after MPs said they were concerned about emissions from a boom in data centres.

item.title: Indian court clears former Delhi chief minister of corruption charges
item.description: Arvind Kejriwal was arrested in March 2024, weeks before India's general election, and spent months in jail.

item.title: Green Party wins Gorton and Denton by-election, pushing Labour into third place
item.description: Labour, which took the seat with more than 50% of the vote in 2024, came behind Reform UK.

item.title: Dyson settles forced labour suit in landmark UK case
item.description: Migrant workers alleged they were subjected to abusive treatment in a Malaysian factory for Dyson.

item.title: 'Shirtless' protest at AI summit sparks political storm in India
item.description: Eight members of the youth wing of India's Congress party have been arrested in connection with the protests.

item.title: 'They seized everything': Yemen's Houthi rebels drive aid groups to the brink
item.description: Aid groups - a lifeline for millions of Yemenis - are being squeezed out of existence in areas under the control of the rebels, the BBC hears.

item.title: The AI videos supercharging Russia's online disinformation campaigns
item.description: Security experts have warned that Western governments are poorly equipped to counter a new frontier of online disinformation.

item.title: India and Israel pledge to boost ties in defence and technology
item.description: Opposition parties have criticised Modi's visit and accused the government of abandoning the Palestinian cause.

item.title: Chris Mason on 'an extraordinary by-election result'
item.description: Chris Mason reports from Manchester on the Gorton and Denton by-election result.

item.title: Epstein tried to buy a palace in Morocco days before his arrest in 2019
item.description: The disgraced financier was in talks to buy luxury abode Bin Ennakhi - but the purchase was never completed.

item.title: Christian groups 'outraged' at Reform conference held in Church House
item.description: A number of Christian groups said the party's immigration policies were opposed to Church beliefs and teachings.

item.title: China removes nine military officials ahead of key political meeting
item.description: No official reason for the removal was given, but it comes ahead of the country's key Two Sessions meeting.

item.title: Australian comedian Magda Szubanski in remission from cancer
item.description: The actress announced last year that she was undergoing treatment for a rare and aggressive cancer.

item.title: American citizen among those killed in Cuba boat shooting, US official says
item.description: Cuba has accused those on board of planning "an infiltration with terrorist aims” and firing first.

item.title: The Boys' Love at the heart of Heated Rivalry is a familiar tale in Asia
item.description: Same-sex romance has flourished in Asia as a haven of fantasy and escapism for women and the queer community.

item.title: Hong Kong jailed my dad to stop me speaking out, says activist
item.description: Pro-democracy activist Anna Kwok's father has been jailed for eight months for a national security offence.

item.title: Remains found in Tasmania most likely belong to missing Belgian backpacker
item.description: Celine Cremer, 31, disappeared after going on a hike in a rainforest in June 2023.

item.title: MoD launches review into whether Epstein used RAF bases
item.description: Records and emails will be reviewed to determine whether the convicted sex offender used military bases when visiting the UK.

item.title: Mandelson referred to EU anti-fraud agency over Epstein emails
item.description: The European Commission says it is assessing whether the peer breached its code of conduct while its trade envoy.

item.title: Man, 82, crushed to death in car at Munich scrapyard
item.description: Police said the car was meant to be scrapped and the man must have climbed into it without being noticed.

item.title: EU states told to use existing fund for safe abortions after citizens' petition
item.description: The European Commission stops short of creating a new funding facility to enable women to travel for safe abortions.

item.title: No 10 will have no say on which Mandelson papers are released
item.description: A committee of MPs and peers says the government has confirmed it will not overrule any decision to disclose files.

item.title: Kenyan charged with luring young men to fight for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: A total of 1,000 Kenyans have reportedly been recruited to fight for Russia in its war against Ukraine.

item.title: Spain to check Gibraltar arrivals under post-Brexit deal
item.description: Arrivals at the Rock's airport would have to show their passport twice under a new UK-EU treaty.

item.title: Zimbabwe eliminated from World Cup after defeat to India
item.description: Defending champions India pile on 256-4 as they eliminate Zimbabwe from the T20 World Cup with victory in Chennai.

item.title: What North Korea’s mysterious party congress reveals
item.description: Kim Jong Un uses a five-yearly gathering to boost his daughter, and more

item.title: Four convicted over spyware scandal that shook Greece
item.description: In what became known as "Greece's Watergate", surveillance software called Predator was used to target 87 people.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine exchange more than 1,000 soldiers' bodies
item.description: Russia's announcement comes as Ukraine's top negotiator met US peace envoys in Geneva.

item.title: South Africa stay unbeaten with win over West Indies
item.description: Aiden Markram smacks 82 not out off 46 balls as South Africa beat West Indies by nine wickets to maintain their unbeaten record at the 2026 T20 World Cup

item.title: World Economic Forum boss quits after review of Epstein links
item.description: Brende acknowledged communications with Epstein but said he was "completely unaware" of his past criminal activity.

item.title: Girl, 14, shot dead as South Africa's 'taxi wars' hit school
item.description: Violence has plagued South Africa's highly competitive and lucrative taxi industry for decades.

item.title: Corbyn set to become Your Party parliamentary leader
item.description: Ex-Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn's supporters won 14 of the 24 seats on Your Party's central executive committee (CEC).

item.title: Danish PM calls snap election with Greenland issue centre-stage
item.description: The issue of Greenland, which Donald Trump wants to annex, is likely to dominate the election campaign.

item.title: How the war in Ukraine affects Siberian Russia
item.description: It has changed Yakutia, but not its core

item.title: Ukraine is scaling up interceptor drones
item.description: Machines that kill are the next frontier

item.title: Giorgia Meloni is taking on the courts in Italy
item.description: Italy’s referendum will do nothing to speed up its sluggish judiciary

item.description: But the cost at home for Iran’s proxy in Lebanon will be high

item.title: South Sudan’s decrepit regime is unravelling
item.description: It is taking the country down with it

item.title: Iranians’ angry defiance is growing once again
item.description: Before missiles were launched, the regime was making few concessions

item.title: Ali Larijani is an increasingly plausible heir in Iran
item.description: But hardliners have never fully trusted him

item.title: Google Maps makes another pitch for better South Korean data
item.description: Now Donald Trump is on Google’s side

item.title: China piles pressure on Japan after Takaichi Sanae’s triumph
item.description: The latest feud between the East Asian giants is unlikely to end soon

item.title: Donald Trump’s oil embargo reveals a solar boom in Cuba
item.description: Chinese solar panels are flooding into the troubled island nation

item.title: Mapping China’s holiday rush
item.description: How travel during the Spring Festival is changing

item.title: America’s states should beware of copying Europe too much
item.description: Welfare is rightly becoming more generous. But regulatory fragmentation is a problem

item.title: Heathrow’s third runway is turning into another infrastructure fiasco
item.description: The government must step in

item.title: Donald Trump is at risk of launching a war without purpose
item.description: A conflict with Iran without a clear objective would be recklessly dangerous

item.title: Is Ethiopia heading back to war in Tigray?
item.description: Many people are again leaving the region of Tigray just over three years after the civil war there ended.

item.title: First writing may be 40,000 years earlier than thought
item.description: Scientists believe they have found evidence of written thoughts of Stone Age people on ancient objects.

item.title: Orban steps up oil accusations against Ukraine after vetoing EU loan to Kyiv
item.description: Hungary's prime minister accuses Ukraine of imposing an "oil blockade" after Kyiv says a critical pipeline was closed following Russian strikes.

item.title: 'People are getting poorer': How Iran's struggling economy is changing how families live
item.description: The soaring cost of living has left people struggling to afford basic items - creating pressures which led to mass protests.

item.title: Nigeria to hold inquest into death of Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's toddler
item.description: The internationally acclaimed writer has accused Nigerian hospital Euracare of negligence, which it denies.

item.title: SOS for India’s Pink City
item.description: A threat from UNESCO might prod Jaipur into saving its heritage

item.title: America’s dangerous pursuit of critical-mineral dominance
item.description: With a more focused approach, it could break China’s chokehold

item.title: 'I can't breathe any more': Inside the night a mob burned a newspaper
item.description: A journalist recounts the night a mob torched the Daily Star, trapping 28 staff on the roof.

item.title: Nepal election 2026: When is the vote and who are the main candidates?
item.description: This election will be the first since deadly youth protests toppled the government in 2025.

item.title: Shot in school uniform: BBC reveals police order led to Gen Z protest killings
item.description: New evidence reveals what happened when 19 people were shot dead in Kathmandu last September.

item.title: Why Asian firms are not cheering Trump tariff ruling
item.description: The US Supreme Court's decision to block a pillar of US trade policy has caused yet more uncertainty.

item.title: The right response to private-market dangers
item.description: Was a Blue Owl fund mismanaged, or did it reveal fundamental problems about the industry?

item.title: Watch: BBC on streets of Mexican city gripped by deadly cartel violence
item.description: BBC international correspondent Quentin Sommerville travelled to Culiacán in northern Sinaloa state following an explosion in violence.

item.description: How to trade cachet for dollars and yuan

item.title: American labs say China’s AI tigers are copycats
item.description: DeepSeek’s new model has American officials and firms on edge

item.title: Politician brothers convicted in Brazil for ordering murder of prominent councillor
item.description: Marielle Franco's murder at the age of 38 in Rio de Janeiro sparked protests across Brazil.

item.description: The president himself may be among them

item.title: Spain declassifies files on 1981 attempted coup in effort to dispel conspiracy theories
item.description: The release comes on the same day as the death of the coup leader, Antonio Tejero Molina.

item.title: 'Fear is everywhere': BBC reports from Mexican city turned into war zone by drug cartel feud
item.description: Culiacán in northern Mexico has seen a surge in violence as rival factions of the Sinaloa cartel battle for control.

item.title: Modernisation is making South-East Asia more Islamic
item.description: Economic growth in Malaysia and Indonesia has not led to secularism

item.title: Man Utd ruled out signing Osimhen 'because of Afcon'
item.description: The Africa Cup of Nations proved to be "a big stumbling block" when Manchester United discussed signing Victor Osimhen, according to former coach Benni McCarthy.

item.title: Donald Trump’s State of the Union Was Long and Wrong
item.description: But at least the President thinks everything is going great.

item.title: Brazil floods: 'I saw people trapped in their homes crying for help'
item.description: Dozens of people have died in landslides and flooding in the Brazilian state of Minas Gerais.

item.title: Modi's Israel visit to test India's priorities in the Middle East
item.description: India and Israel's relations have expanded since Modi came to power more than a decade ago.

item.title: Joshua crash driver case adjourned to March
item.description: The driver of the car involved in a fatal crash involving British heavyweight boxer Anthony Joshua has appeared in court in Nigeria.

item.title: BBC sees damage in Puerto Vallarta after Mexican cartel violence
item.description: BBC's Will Grant visits Puerto Vallarta, a popular coastal resort town, to see the damage.

item.title: Threat of further violence looms after Mexican cartel rampage
item.description: The BBC speaks to Guadalajara residents days after cartel members sought to avenge the killing of Nemesio "El Mencho" Oseguera.

item.title: Drones hammer Sudan's gold and oil zone - the pivotal new front line
item.description: Intensified attacks in the Kordofan region have led to mass casualties as peace remains elusive.

item.description: From the daily newsletter: our political columnist previews the President’s State of the Union address.

item.title: Floods and landslides in Brazil kill at least 25
item.description: Hundreds of people have been displaced and dozens are missing, with rescue operations still ongoing in Juiz de Fora.

item.title: Brazil’s high court is caught up in a vast scandal
item.description: Some of the world’s most powerful judges have an over-cosy relationship with business and political elites

item.description: John McDermott, our chief Africa correspondent, talks to Hakainde Hichilema

item.title: Why did Putin's Russia invade Ukraine?
item.description: Russia hoped to overthrow Ukraine's pro-Western government, but Moscow's war has dragged on for more than four years.

item.title: How to get rich in modern China
item.description: Some of the country’s brightest are cashing in on a state-backed surge
